the CMS mostly, are happy with the product or using it at all or what issues do they have?

There was a handful of things that I noticed here, though.
One, this, the original title here was not really semantically correct.
So keep an eye out for that.
LLMs do this weird thing sometimes where they'll generate titles that aren't actually correct from an English language perspective.
And so you have to, like, just make sure you say it out loud in your head before you publish, because Lover's Beach Cabo, seven best things you can do there is not actually semantically correct.
So I just really just rotated this to be semantically accurate.
There is a second.
In this case, this title is probably fine with a colon, but I want to keep an eye out for LLM's tendency to rely on headlines with colons in them.
It really, really, really loves them.
Most LLMs really love them for some reason.
I don't know why.
It's probably some sort of training data set that they've got over-indexed on.
But the vast majority of headlines almost never meet a colon.
It's just not really necessary.
I would say it's a fraction of the overall that actually needs it.
this case, it's probably fine.
I feel like maybe they do it because to keep the keyword right in the start.
Yeah, sometimes they can make for awkward positioning to keyword stuff at the beginning of the sentence.
But usually there are ways to like, because I can rewrite this without a colon of four different ways.
There are ways to keep the early on and yet not rely on a colon, you know?
So just flagging.
That's something to watch out for if you can, and you can tell it, hey, keep the keyword early, just add it in your polishing instructions.
It's totally fine to keep the keyword early, but avoid using a colon.
Thank you, right?
Colons and commas are generally bad in headlines, if you can avoid them.
The vast majority of this stuff is just fine.

This section here on the seasons, so this is kind of an easy win, and this is one of those things where, you know, there's a couple of things going on in this section that I think are worth talking about and worth paying attention to and keeping an eye on.
One, single sentence paragraphs are a big LLM crutch when you give it instructions about, that include words like brevity or clarity or conciseness or, you know, make this concise, keep it brief.
It's like, cool, I'm going to give you one sentence, right?
It loves a single sentence or a bullet list.
We know this.
They have a predilection for it.
So keep an eye out for those.

Generally speaking, if you get a block of single sentences, that's an opportunity to go back either into the polishing instructions or the workflow or an LLM box and run it again and say, hey, this information seems sound.
Could you build a narrative out of this for me?
And then, of course, the asterisk here would be if the client is really in love with single sentences or bullet points or whatever.
You know, the client needs to supersede anything I'm saying here or their explicit desires.
But it does not mean you can't push back and say, hey, I know you love bullet points, but I think this is a really good opportunity to expand this a little bit.
So, you know, that's the client is always right until they're not.
Right.
That's the way I look at it.
And we ultimately are not responsible for the thing we're most responsible for is outcomes, not matching what they think is the right thing to do.
It is really up to us to go like, hey, this content.
We'll perform better if X.
And if they're like, oh, that's cool, but we don't like it, great.
You've said your piece and you've tried it best for what they want, you know.
So it's a little bit of a push and pull.
Generally speaking, I think it's important to remember that clients, the vast majority of our clients, depending on the exact stakeholder level, they're not content writers.
They're not writers.
They're not editors.
They don't understand the process of creating content.
don't understand the, like, nuances of it.
They are, in general, going to be most interested in outcomes.
And the opinions they have about content are often wrong or misguided.
And your job is to not then, like, spit on that, but then to find a way to, like, get them to a place where they're, like, you know, understanding, hey, maybe it would be better if we did this.
Maybe it would be better if we did this.
It's a little bit of a narrative, that sort of thing.
I've been telling my team, like, we are in content creation and we are, like, we are in marketing, but, like, it's.
The base of it, we are in customer service.
So we have to walk that line of like, there is a customer here, and we have to pander to that a little bit.
Yeah, I like to think of it as like you're dealing with your team's most ego-driven writer, where you have to come at it and like, you can't make hard edits on them because they have an ego, right?
You have to go like, hey.
If you thought about, what if?
Right, exactly.

want the weather to be nice, all this stuff.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Content audit review and discussion of workflow improvements for GrowthX's content creation process.](https://fathom.video/share/iCDMCUnG_YdJBysRmwDeLZsJnYLszYFr?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [LLM-generated content needs improvement in human readability and narrative structure](https://fathom.video/share/iCDMCUnG_YdJBysRmwDeLZsJnYLszYFr?tab=summary&timestamp=69.0)
  - [Current workflows (e.g., AirOps) have limitations in fact-checking, linking, and tone calibration](https://fathom.video/share/iCDMCUnG_YdJBysRmwDeLZsJnYLszYFr?tab=summary&timestamp=132.0)
  - [Transition to sprint model will lead to more customized, client-specific workflows and polishing instructions](https://fathom.video/share/iCDMCUnG_YdJBysRmwDeLZsJnYLszYFr?tab=summary&timestamp=1000.0)
  - [Need for better fact-checking processes, especially for clients like InterVest dealing with health-related topics](https://fathom.video/share/iCDMCUnG_YdJBysRmwDeLZsJnYLszYFr?tab=summary&timestamp=1396.0)

## Topics

### Content Quality and Readability

  - [LLMs tend to rely on "bullet point sandwiches" and single-sentence paragraphs](https://fathom.video/share/iCDMCUnG_YdJBysRmwDeLZsJnYLszYFr?tab=summary&timestamp=339.0)
  - [Need for varied paragraph structures and narrative flow to improve readability](https://fathom.video/share/iCDMCUnG_YdJBysRmwDeLZsJnYLszYFr?tab=summary&timestamp=799.0)
  - [Importance of critical thinking in expanding thin sections (e.g., weather information for travel content)](https://fathom.video/share/iCDMCUnG_YdJBysRmwDeLZsJnYLszYFr?tab=summary&timestamp=529.0)
  - [Moving away from word count as a metric; focus on "as long as it needs to be and then slightly shorter"](https://fathom.video/share/iCDMCUnG_YdJBysRmwDeLZsJnYLszYFr?tab=summary&timestamp=647.0)

### Workflow and Tool Usage

  - [AirOps used for some clients (InterVest, HomeBase, Tribe) but has limitations in research, structuring, and linking](https://fathom.video/share/iCDMCUnG_YdJBysRmwDeLZsJnYLszYFr?tab=summary&timestamp=1134.0)
  - [ChatGPT preferred for certain clients (e.g., Seat) due to faster calibration and familiarity with client needs](https://fathom.video/share/iCDMCUnG_YdJBysRmwDeLZsJnYLszYFr?tab=summary&timestamp=1529.0)
  - [Assignment brief and feedback column in content grids have been helpful improvements](https://fathom.video/share/iCDMCUnG_YdJBysRmwDeLZsJnYLszYFr?tab=summary&timestamp=1200.0)
  - [Need for custom workflows and polishing instructions per client, to be developed during sprint phases](https://fathom.video/share/iCDMCUnG_YdJBysRmwDeLZsJnYLszYFr?tab=summary&timestamp=995.0)

### Client-Specific Challenges

  - [Seat: Moving from short-form, scannable content to longer-form, more digestible content](https://fathom.video/share/iCDMCUnG_YdJBysRmwDeLZsJnYLszYFr?tab=summary&timestamp=1512.0)
  - [InterVest: Programmatic content generation requires significant post-editing (about 1 hour per piece)](https://fathom.video/share/iCDMCUnG_YdJBysRmwDeLZsJnYLszYFr?tab=summary&timestamp=1337.0)
  - [Reddit: Ongoing issues with multiple rounds of manual edits, potentially exceeding contract terms](https://fathom.video/share/iCDMCUnG_YdJBysRmwDeLZsJnYLszYFr?tab=summary&timestamp=1605.0)

### Future Improvements

  - [Develop fact-checking workflows, especially for health-related content (e.g., InterVest)](https://fathom.video/share/iCDMCUnG_YdJBysRmwDeLZsJnYLszYFr?tab=summary&timestamp=1396.0)
  - [Improve linking capabilities in content generation tools](https://fathom.video/share/iCDMCUnG_YdJBysRmwDeLZsJnYLszYFr?tab=summary&timestamp=1500.0)
  - [Retcon improvements for existing clients based on new client onboarding processes](https://fathom.video/share/iCDMCUnG_YdJBysRmwDeLZsJnYLszYFr?tab=summary&timestamp=1484.0)
  - [Design transition process for handing off clients from sprint teams to regular pods](https://fathom.video/share/iCDMCUnG_YdJBysRmwDeLZsJnYLszYFr?tab=summary&timestamp=1465.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Review and address ongoing issues with Reddit account](https://fathom.video/share/iCDMCUnG_YdJBysRmwDeLZsJnYLszYFr?tab=summary&timestamp=1605.0)
  - [Explore development of fact-checking workflows, particularly for InterVest](https://fathom.video/share/iCDMCUnG_YdJBysRmwDeLZsJnYLszYFr?tab=summary&timestamp=1396.0)
  - [Consider retconning improvements to existing client processes based on new sprint model insights](https://fathom.video/share/iCDMCUnG_YdJBysRmwDeLZsJnYLszYFr?tab=summary&timestamp=1484.0)
  - [Continue to refine and customize workflows and polishing instructions per client](https://fathom.video/share/iCDMCUnG_YdJBysRmwDeLZsJnYLszYFr?tab=summary&timestamp=995.0)

action_items.description: Review polishing instructions for InterVest, HomeBase, Tribe. Adjust to reduce bullet point sandwiches, improve linking, enhance fact-checking.
